header.description: Post, like, and search tweets, send messages, search users, and add users to lists

directMessage.description: Send a direct message to a user

list.description: Add a user to a list

tweet.description: Create, like, search, or delete a tweet

create.description: Create, quote, or reply to a tweet

add.description: Add a member to a list

search.description: Search for tweets from the last seven days

user.description: The user you want to search

text.description: The text of the status update. URLs must be encoded. Links wrapped with the t.co shortener will affect character count

attachments.description: The attachment ID to associate with the message

sortOrder.description: The order in which to return results

startTime.description: Tweets before this date will not be returned. This date must be within the last 7 days if you don't have Academic Research access.

endTime.description: Tweets after this date will not be returned. This date must be within the last 7 days if you don't have Academic Research access.

tweetFieldsObject.description: The fields to add to each returned tweet object. Default fields are: ID, text, edit_history_tweet_ids.

list.description: The list you want to add the user to

noticeLocation.displayName: Locations are not supported due to Twitter V2 API limitations

noticeAttachments.displayName: Attachements are not supported due to Twitter V2 API limitations

searchText.description: A UTF-8, URL-encoded search query of 500 characters maximum, including operators. Queries may additionally be limited by complexity. Check the searching examples here.

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

me.description: Whether you want to search the authenticated user
